$comment: Tier-2 DB-state fixture spec for the `doc_mount_folders` repo — a mount-index
sibling-DB repo of Phase 2 (after the pilot `group_character_members`). One
committed file drives BOTH sides of the differential:
- the Rust port (quilltap-harness doc_mount_folders tier-2 test).
SIBLING DB: in v4 this table lives in quilltap-mount-index.db (NOT the main
DB). The builder/oracle point SQLITE_MOUNT_INDEX_PATH at the fixture and read
it back through getRawMountIndexDatabase(); the Rust Writer simply opens that
file by path. This corpus banks a NULLABLE-UUID column (parentId) in a
sibling-DB repo: a ROOT folder has parentId = null, a CHILD folder points its
parentId at the root's id. mountPointId is a required UUID-as-TEXT ref stored
as plain TEXT (no FK constraint); name + path are required TEXT.
SAFETY: testPepperBase64 is a THROWAWAY 32-byte key, the same one every other
tier-2 fixture uses. It is NOT the real Friday pepper and is safe to commit.
DETERMINISM: every id and timestamp is pinned, so both sides produce
byte-identical post-op state and the canonical dump needs ZERO normalization.

ops.comment: Add a second child under the root (non-null parentId path).
Rename the 'Notes' child and change its path. createdAt preserved, updatedAt pinned.
